retry_reason: Failure (NULL) means the ordered extent is not currently present; it may be inserted later (e.g., after a concurrent write starts), so the caller can retry when the condition changes.
contract_summary: This function looks up a btrfs_ordered_extent by file offset in the inode's ordered tree and acquires a reference on it via refcount_inc. It returns the ordered extent with an incremented refcount, or NULL if not found. The caller MUST check the return value and must call btrfs_put_ordered_extent to drop the reference when done. On a NULL return, the caller may retry later because the ordered extent may become available (e.g., after an ordered write begins). The function holds the ordered_tree_lock only during the lookup, ensuring safe access to the tree.

judge.reason: The function's primary purpose is to look up a btrfs_ordered_extent by file offset and acquire a reference (refcount_inc) on it before returning. The only additional logic is the tree search, range check, and a tracepoint, all of which are directly subordinate to the lookup-and-ref operation.

check.reason: refcount_inc is called on &entry->refs, where entry is the returned pointer. The access path entry.refs.refs traces from the return value through struct btrfs_ordered_extent field refs (refcount_t) to its inner atomic_t field refs. The type chain btrfs_ordered_extent--refcount_t--atomic_t is consistent with kernel definitions. Both direction (get) and location (return, index 0) are correct.
